judge.validator_python:
def validate(args, output):
    expression = args[0]
    if not isinstance(output, str):
        return False
    if output.count('(') != 1 or output.count(')') != 1:
        return False
    idx_open = output.find('(')
    idx_close = output.find(')')
    idx_plus = output.find('+')
    if idx_plus == -1 or not (idx_open < idx_plus < idx_close):
        return False
    if idx_plus - idx_open - 1 < 1 or idx_close - idx_plus - 1 < 1:
        return False
    stripped = output[:idx_open] + output[idx_open + 1:idx_close] + output[idx_close + 1:]
    if stripped != expression:
        return False
    prefix = output[:idx_open]
    inside_left = output[idx_open + 1:idx_plus]
    inside_right = output[idx_plus + 1:idx_close]
    suffix = output[idx_close + 1:]
    left_mult = int(prefix) if prefix else 1
    right_mult = int(suffix) if suffix else 1
    value = left_mult * (int(inside_left) + int(inside_right)) * right_mult
    plus_at = expression.index('+')
    num1 = expression[:plus_at]
    num2 = expression[plus_at + 1:]
    best = None
    for i in range(len(num1)):
        p = num1[:i]
        il = num1[i:]
        for j in range(1, len(num2) + 1):
            ir = num2[:j]
            s = num2[j:]
            lm = int(p) if p else 1
            rm = int(s) if s else 1
            v = lm * (int(il) + int(ir)) * rm
            if best is None or v < best:
                best = v
    return value == best

pattern: The parenthesized pair can only slide across one internal split point in each half of the expression, so trying every (left-split, right-split) combination and multiplying the leftover prefix/suffix around the parenthesized sum finds the true minimum directly -- no expression parsing beyond that.
hints: The parentheses must straddle the '+', so really you're only choosing two things: how much of the left number stays outside (a prefix) and how much of the right number stays outside (a suffix).

For a fixed split, the value is (left prefix, or 1 if empty) times (the two inner pieces added together) times (right suffix, or 1 if empty) -- so just try every split of the left number and every split of the right number and evaluate that formula.

Don't assume wrapping the whole expression in one big pair of parentheses is always best -- a large leftover suffix acting as a multiplier can make a partial split much smaller; you have to compare every combination and keep the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, splitting both numbers differently
statement example, best answer wraps the entire expression
shortest possible expression, one digit each side
longest possible expression, single-digit right operand
the minimum requires a partial split on both sides, not wrapping the whole thing
wrapping the entire expression looks tempting but a partial split with a small multiplier beats it
symmetric digit strings on both sides of the plus

stress.description: random digit strings (never containing zero) around the plus, spanning the full length range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total_len = rng.randint(3, 10)
    num1_len = rng.randint(1, total_len - 2)
    num2_len = total_len - 1 - num1_len
    num1 = ''.join(str(rng.randint(1, 9)) for _ in range(num1_len))
    num2 = ''.join(str(rng.randint(1, 9)) for _ in range(num2_len))
    return [num1 + '+' + num2]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minimizeResult(self, expression):
        plus_at = expression.index('+')
        num1 = expression[:plus_at]
        num2 = expression[plus_at + 1:]
        candidates = []
        for i in range(len(num1)):
            prefix = num1[:i]
            inside_left = num1[i:]
            for j in range(1, len(num2) + 1):
                inside_right = num2[:j]
                suffix = num2[j:]
                left_mult = int(prefix) if prefix else 1
                right_mult = int(suffix) if suffix else 1
                value = left_mult * (int(inside_left) + int(inside_right)) * right_mult
                text = prefix + '(' + inside_left + '+' + inside_right + ')' + suffix
                candidates.append((value, text))
        best_value = min(v for v, _ in candidates)
        for value, text in candidates:
            if value == best_value:
                return text
        return candidates[0][1]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minimizeResult(self, expression):
        plus_at = expression.index('+')
        num1 = expression[:plus_at]
        num2 = expression[plus_at + 1:]
        best_value = None
        best_text = None
        for i in range(len(num1)):
            prefix = num1[:i]
            inside_left = num1[i:]
            for j in range(1, len(num2) + 1):
                inside_right = num2[:j]
                suffix = num2[j:]
                left_mult = int(prefix) if prefix else 1
                right_mult = int(suffix) if suffix else 1
                value = left_mult * (int(inside_left) + int(inside_right)) * right_mult
                if best_value is None or value < best_value:
                    best_value = value
                    best_text = prefix + '(' + inside_left + '+' + inside_right + ')' + suffix
        return best_text
